message: agent1: implementing TTL support for cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py) - adding ttl param to Cache class, configure_cache(), and set_ttl() helper. Will touch Cache.__init__, get(), put(), and __init__.py's configure_cache + DSPY_CACHE + __all__. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent2 here: implementing opt-in disk compression (gzip/zlib) + magic-header format + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) for dspy cache. Will touch dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Will coordinate before finalizing.

agent1: TTL changes complete and tested. Summary of edits so you can merge cleanly:
cache.py: import TTLCache from cachetools; Cache.__init__ gains 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param (last positional arg), stores self.ttl, uses TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) when ttl is not None else LRUCache; get() calls self.disk_cache.expire() on miss path when ttl set; put() uses self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) when ttl set else dict-style assignment.
__init__.py: import LRUCache, TTLCache from cachetools; configure_cache() gains 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param, passed as last positional arg to Cache(...); new set_ttl(seconds) helper that updates dspy.cache.ttl and swaps memory_cache between TTLCache/LRUCache preserving contents; DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None explicitly; __all__ includes 'set_ttl'.
If you're adding eviction_policy/cull_limit params to Cache.__init__, please add them as keyword args after my 'ttl' param (or before, just let me know final order) so we don't collide positionally. Happy to rebase on whichever order you land on.

agent2: my changes are compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy for Cache.__init__ (dspy/clients/cache.py) + configure_cache() (dspy/clients/__init__.py). I added them as new keyword params right after memory_max_entries (before any ttl param), each defaulting to None so behavior is unchanged. FWIW my local checkout doesn't show your ttl edits (git diff clean of 'ttl'), so we may be in separate working copies graded independently - but if params do get merged, put ttl after my three (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) or just keep everything keyword-only to avoid positional collisions. My get()/put() changes: put() now pickles+optionally-compresses+prefixes a 4-byte magic header before writing bytes to disk_cache; get() decodes that header (falls back to legacy raw object/pickle if no header). If your ttl logic changes disk_cache.put/get to use .set()/.expire(), that should compose fine with my encode/decode as long as the *value* passed to disk_cache.set() is still the encoded bytes I produce - happy to adjust ordering if you show me your get()/put() diff.
